title: Crucible v1: typed prompts + tool calls eDSL
description: Exploration/learning eDSL inspired by BAML + 12-factor-agents. effectful substrate, own schema+SAP parser, Anthropic+scripted+recording interpreters. See docs/specs/2026-06-05-crucible-design.md

JSON (Value/Parse/Encode/Decode), Schema+Codec(+Generic), SAP (stripToJson/decodeLLM), Decision+reduce, effectful LLM/Tools substrate with scripted + LIVE Anthropic interpreters + recordLLM cassettes, example tool-using agent, and the eval harness. Live Anthropic call verified (M8). effectful migration done (crucible-hhk). Built on zinc with the full http-client-tls cascade now compiling.

title: Bootstrap confidence intervals on calibration kappa (from autorubric review)
description: Pure addition to Crucible.Eval.Calibrate: kappaCI :: (Double, Double) on CalibrationReport, 95% bootstrap over the (human, judge) pairs with a seeded generator (resample, recompute kappa, take 2.5/97.5 percentiles); renderCalibration prints it next to kappa. Rationale: ~30 labels is a small sample; a wide CI means the kappa should not be trusted yet. No LLM calls. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-autorubric-review.md item 4.

title: Provider fallback + round-robin interpreter combinators (from BAML review)
description: Compose the Anthropic/OpenAI interpreters at the runEff edge with BAML-matching semantics: retry within a member via its isRetryable, then advance; composite-level policy retries the whole chain. Include collector-grade per-call logs (timing, attempt, selected provider) layered over Usage, which matters once fallback exists. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-baml-review.md.

title: Few-shot calibrated judging (from autorubric review)
description: Feed verdict-balanced labelled examples (which calibrate already collects) into the judge prompt: judgeOnceWith :: [JudgeExample] -> ..., pure seeded balanceExamples, and a calibrate variant that holds out the measurement split. The one autorubric feature with published effect sizes (RiceChem 77.2->80.0 p=0.023; cookbook 75->90). See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-autorubric-review.md.
close_reason: Shipped: JudgeOpts/JudgeExample/balanceExamples/judgePrompt, judgeWith/runEvalWith generalizations, calibrateWith structural holdout (exampleCount/measured on the report), 10 hermetic tests, evals.md workflow updated.

title: Review: latest BAML for concepts to add to crucible
description: Survey BAML (boundaryml) docs/changelog over the last 6 months: client retry/fallback policies, semantic streaming of partial structured outputs, assert/check output constraints, dynamic types, collectors/usage, test blocks beyond what crucible took. Outcome: research note with a ranked list of crucible candidates + follow-up feature beads.
close_reason: Note: docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-baml-review.md. BAML 0.222/0.12: client fallback+round-robin, semantic streaming attributes over Partial types, assert/check refinements, TypeBuilder, Collector, multimodal. Crucible keeps the reverse edge (eval stack, cassettes, improveSkill). Follow-up beads filed for the top additions.

title: Review: autorubric vs crucible Eval (gap analysis)
description: Read the Autorubric paper (arXiv 2603.00077) and its library if public. Table: what autorubric does that crucible's Eval/Judge/Calibrate/Grounding does not (ordinal criteria? judge ensembles across model families? few-shot calibration into the judge prompt? rubric generation?), what crucible already does better, and a ranked list of additions. Outcome: research note + follow-up feature beads.
close_reason: Note: docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-autorubric-review.md. Autorubric v1.5: weighted criteria incl. negative, judge ensembles, few-shot verdict-balanced calibration (published gains), CANNOT_ASSESS, kappa/ICC/EMD/bootstrap. Its binary-vs-ordinal data (87% vs 38-58%) vindicates crucible's binary rule; Grounded has no counterpart. Follow-up beads filed for the top 3 additions.

title: Research: agentic memory architectures
description: Web survey (last 6 months + core papers: MemGPT, Generative Agents reflection, A-MEM, HippoRAG, Mem0, Zep/Graphiti) of memory for LLM agents: episodic/semantic split, consolidation, retrieval, forgetting, sleep-time compute. Outcome: research note with recommendations for what a Memory effect/interpreters could look like in crucible.
close_reason: Note: docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-agentic-memory.md. Three separable policies (gated write, background consolidation, budgeted retrieval); versioned files beat memory services for coding agents; typed memories give free staleness (codec no longer decodes = stale); poisoning mitigated by provenance + write gates. Crucible fit: small Memory effect + consolidation-as-Skill + ablation eval hooks.

title: Research: multi-agent harnesses (gastown and peers)
description: Web survey (last 6 months + core papers AutoGen/MetaGPT/ChatDev, Anthropic multi-agent posts) of multi-agent orchestration harnesses: Steve Yegge's Gas Town (and its beads integration), claude-flow, LangGraph, CrewAI, swarm patterns, controller/subagent designs, mailboxes/handoffs. Outcome: research note with recommendations for crucible's potential harness layer.
close_reason: Note: docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-multi-agent-harnesses.md. Surviving design: orchestrator-worker, isolated child contexts, result-only returns; Gas Town's insight is the persistent merge-friendly work ledger + agent identity; MAST: 21.3% of failures are missing verification (the case for judge gates). Crucible fit: Spawn effect with codec-typed handoffs, judge-gated outputs, ledger, budgets; NOT a process manager or group chat.

title: Research: llm-wiki concepts (agent-maintained wiki knowledge substrates)
description: Web survey (last 6 months + core older papers) of wiki-structured knowledge for LLM agents: agent-maintained wikis, document/page-based memory, claim pages with links and revisions, GraphRAG-adjacent structures. Outcome: research note in docs/superpowers/research/ with recommendations for crucible.
close_reason: Note: docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-llm-wiki.md. Karpathy's llm-wiki gist as reference design (immutable sources, LLM-owned page layers, git as provenance); files+grep empirically strong (Letta 74% LoCoMo); editing discipline: create-at-recurrence, supersede-not-overwrite, delta edits. Crucible fit: Wiki effect + grounding-gated writes via Eval.Grounding.

title: Evals manual: grounding criteria guidance for context-receiving skills
description: Decision from reasoning-trap research review (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-reasoning-trap.md, point 3a). Add a 'grounding criteria' subsection to the evals manual rubric guidance: skills whose output reasons over provided material (RAG, summarisation, analysis, classification-with-rationale) must include binary grounding criteria — 'every factual claim is supported by the provided context', 'quotes/cites the source span for key claims', negative form 'does not introduce facts absent from the context'. Rationale: accuracy-only evals are blind to grounding decay (Reasoning Trap: 88% accuracy retained with 43% faithfulness loss). Scope limit: only where context exists; creative skills skip. Doubles as verbosity-bias hardening.
close_reason: Grounding-criteria section added to docs/evals.md (binary supported-claims/cited-spans/no-invented-facts criteria for context-receiving skills, creative skills exempt, verbosity-hardening note) + at-a-glance rule 6.

title: renderReport: vote tally + majority-side rationale label for judgeN
description: Decision from reasoning-trap research review (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-reasoning-trap.md, point 2). When judgeN (n>1 majority voting) lands: renderReport shows vote tally (e.g. 'pass 2/3'), flags contested cases (2-1 splits) as judge-uncertain, labels the displayed rationale as 'majority-side rationale (1 of 2)' rather than presenting it as the reason for the verdict, and keeps dissenting rationales accessible in verbose output. Single-call (n=1) rationales keep plain framing. Blocked on judgeN existing.
close_reason: Shipped: VoteOutcome/Score carry dissent; renderReport shows [votes y-f], labels voted rationales as majority-side samples, and inlines dissent on contested cases. Single-call (n=1) rationales keep plain framing. Dissent is accessible programmatically on Score (no separate verbose mode exists; the report inlines it where it matters).

title: Document 'no closed-loop judging' hard rule in evals manual
description: Decision from reasoning-trap research review (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-reasoning-trap.md): any multi-step judge protocol must pass the original skill output and rubric verbatim into every LLM call; never judge from a summary, critique, or transcript of another judge step. Fallback when re-injection is impossible: independent one-shot judges + mechanical vote. Add this rule with the one-line review test ('does every LLM call receive the original output and rubric?') to the evals manual page.
close_reason: Covered by dd2291e: 'No closed-loop judging' section on docs/evals.md with the one-line review test, structural rationale, and judgeN open-loop note; also added to the page's checklist as item 14.

title: Remove manifest mentions from the manual

close_reason: Closed

title: Add test cases to prompts (BAML-style)
description: BAML lets you declare named test cases alongside a prompt/function and run them. Design + implement an analogous crucible feature: attach example inputs (and expected outputs) to a Skill and run them via the eval machinery. Spike scope: API sketch + working minimal version + tests.

Config type, retries, error type, tests with fixture JSON; live verify if OPENAI_API_KEY available.

title: Investigate streaming jsonl / line-by-line for row-based data

Incremental partial-object decode judged out of scope (needs an event-based JSON parser; revisit only if a real consumer needs it). Documented in docs/streaming.md.

title: SP1: bump crucible GHC 9.6.5 -> 9.10.1
description: Platform bump to align with manifest (9.10.1), prerequisite for sharing an autodocodec types package. Update flake.nix (provide ghc9101 + nixpkgs) and zinc.toml ghc pin; rebuild the vendored closure (effectful, http-client-tls+crypton, retry, mlkem/hpke/ram, etc.) under 9.10.1; bump/forward-pin deps as needed, reusing manifest's working 9.10.1 resolution where shared. No behaviour change; success = build + tests green on 9.10.1. Empirical spike-first (discover breakage before planning fixes).
close_reason: Done, clean. GHC 9.6.5->9.10.1: flake 24.05->24.11 + ghc9101 (manifest's nixpkgs rev), zinc.toml ghc=9.10.1. Zero dependency changes (zinc.lock unchanged); full closure rebuilds, tests + live Anthropic path green on 9.10.1. Merged to master (62de4ed). Gating risk for the autodocodec epic cleared.

title: Adopt autodocodec/aeson for manifest interop (shared typed persistence)
description: Epic: converge crucible's domain codec onto autodocodec (what manifest uses) so crucible types are both prompt/tool-schema sources AND manifest-persistable jsonb. Sub-projects: SP1 GHC 9.6.5->9.10.1 bump; SP2 add aeson+autodocodec deps (reuse manifest pins); SP3 migrate Crucible.Codec/Schema -> autodocodec; SP5 shared-types package + manifest persistence link. SP4 (wire-layer Crucible.Json->aeson) optional/deferred. Driver: ecosystem interop with sibling project manifest (../manifest, GHC 9.10.1, autodocodec+aeson+postgres UoW).
notes: SP1 (GHC 9.10.1) DONE 62de4ed. SP2 (aeson+autodocodec deps) DONE 91d5d74. SP3 (full aeson+autodocodec migration; Crucible.Json+Schema deleted; SP4 folded in) DONE+merged 13a06fa — build/suite/live all green. REMAINING: SP5 — shared-types package + manifest persistence link (crucible types now autodocodec-HasCodec, i.e. manifest-ready; the actual entity/Session persistence is the design-heavy payoff, not yet built). Follow-up: crucible has a Maybe-schema test gap bead.

title: A#4: capture token usage and cost from live Anthropic responses
description: Direction A (productionize live path), sub-project 4 - the quick win. Parse the usage object (input_tokens/output_tokens) from Anthropic message responses and surface it to callers of complete/converse. Decide where it lands: a usage accumulator effect, a return-value field, or a logged side-channel. Spans both the text path (anthropicComplete/postMessages) and the chat path (runChatAnthropic). Needs its own brainstorm -> spec -> plan cycle.
close_reason: Shipped: Crucible.Usage (Usage Monoid + estimateCost), parseUsage, accumulator interpreters runLLMAnthropicUsage/runChatAnthropicUsage, demo. Merged to master (55f3b5b). Live-verified: 1270 tokens summed across a tool loop.

close_reason: Eval harness: Case/Expectation (Exactly/Predicate/Rubric), Score, runEval -> Report (pass-rate/mean/per-case), renderReport; LLM-as-judge is a Codec-backed prompt; exact/predicate/judge scoring green on scripted data

description: effectful Tools effect; per-tool effect rows (authority creep = compile error); one 2-3 tool example agent as dogfood + test/eval target.
close_reason: MonadTool + Tool registry + dispatchTools; runAgentT dispatches by name; DemoM carrier satisfies (MonadLLM,MonadTool); example agent runs get_weather/add tools to a final answer on scripted replies; 78 checks green. NB: dependency M8/crucible-iln (live Anthropic interpreter) still open — orthogonal to M9; force-closed.

description: effectful LLM effect; runLLMScripted; runAgent control loop on canned replies; scripted-loop tests.
close_reason: MonadLLM + ScriptedM + runAgent loop (render schema -> decodeLLM -> reduce -> dispatch/halt); scripted tool->answer trajectory tests green

title: M6: Decision unification + pure reduce
description: Decision tool answer codec via oneOfC; pure reduce :: AgentState -> Decision -> Step; tests.
close_reason: Decision tool/answer unification via decisionCodec (oneOfC); pure total reduce -> Step; decode/round-trip/reduce tests green

title: M8: Anthropic interpreter + recordLLM cassettes
description: runLLMAnthropic (HTTP approach decided here: curl shell-out vs minimal client); recordLLM reinterpret writing cassettes; first live run.
notes: 2026-06-08: M8 BLOCKED by zinc REGRESSION zinc-9ac (P0). http-client-tls cascade fully builds+runs in scratch (httptest5: 58 pkgs, tls manager created). BUT the current zinc (b6f063b) REGRESSED subdir-member library builds: crucible (member packages/crucible) fails GHC-82272 'module cannot be found locally' for ALL its modules — even at its committed 0cfecd5 config. Confirmed regression: backup pre-bxw zinc builds crucible (14 pkgs, BUILD=0); current zinc fails. Minimal repro (any members=[packages/foo] workspace) in zinc-9ac. So: backup zinc builds crucible but lacks cascade support; current zinc has cascade but can't build crucible. NEITHER works for M8 until zinc-9ac is fixed. Crucible zinc config reverted to committed (clean). ONCE zinc-9ac LANDS: rebuild zinc, then redo crucible dep setup (vendor hpke-0.1.0; add http-client+http-client-tls+bytestring to packages/crucible/zinc.toml [build.lib] depends; zinc add http-client-tls), build crucible, then implement runLLMAnthropic (Anthropic messages API via http-client-tls: POST v1/messages, headers x-api-key/anthropic-version/content-type, JSON body from Messages, parse content[0].text with Crucible.Json) + recordLLM cassettes + first live run (.env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Reference cascade resolve: /tmp/httptest5.
close_reason: M8 DONE + verified live. Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.runLLMAnthropic discharges the LLM effect against the real Anthropic Messages API (POST /v1/messages) over http-client-tls; request/response JSON via in-repo Crucible.Json codecs (no aeson). recordLLMAnthropic + runLLMCassette implement the record/replay cassette slider. Crucible.Json.Encode now renders whole numbers as integers (max_tokens). First live run returns 'pong'; cassette replay matches live. Committed on branch m8-anthropic (4d68920); full suite green. Enabled by the http-client-tls cascade now building under zinc (zinc-bxw/y24/ngd/22z/ix4/hz2/mmx/9ac).

title: Per-call introspection: CallLog via a logged interpreter wrapper (from BAML review)
description: Collector parity, split out of crucible-3sj: a CallLog list (provider, model, duration, TTFT, usage, attempt number, selected flag) accumulated by a logged-style interpreter wrapper at the runEff edge; Usage stays the cheap default. Becomes important the moment fallback (crucible-3sj) exists, because fallback decisions need to be observable. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-baml-review.md item 4.

title: Docs: dynamic codecs pattern (category-from-database) (from BAML review)
description: Document, do not build: BAML needs TypeBuilder because its types are compiled; crucible codecs are runtime values, so enum (zip labels values) over a list fetched at runtime already works. Add a typed-functions.md subsection showing the runtime-enum / dynamic-schema pattern. See the BAML review note item 6.

title: Design cycle: multimodal inputs (image/PDF content blocks) (from BAML review)
description: Brainstorm-with-user candidate: Message carries Text only; both providers support image/PDF content blocks on the wire. Unlocks document-extraction skills (BAML's best demo category). Larger surface change: Message/Block types, request builders for both providers, cassette format, prompt assembly. Deserves its own spec. See the BAML review note item 5.

title: Per-call introspection: CallLog via a logged interpreter wrapper (from BAML review)
description: Collector parity, split out of crucible-3sj: a CallLog list (provider, model, duration, TTFT, usage, attempt number, selected flag) accumulated by a  interpreter wrapper at the runEff edge; Usage stays the cheap default. Becomes important the moment fallback (crucible-3sj) exists, because fallback decisions need to be observable. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-baml-review.md item 4.
close_reason: Duplicate of crucible-c11 (created twice due to a shell quoting error).

title: Executable rubric lint: lintChecklist (from autorubric review)
description: lintChecklist :: (LLM :> es) => [Criterion] -> Eff es [Text] running the evals-page four-check walk as an advisory LLM call over criterion labels: double-barreled 'and' criteria (conflation), direction ambiguity, near-duplicates (weight double-counting), plus vague/unfalsifiable wording from autorubric's meta-rubric anti-patterns. Advisory only, never a gate. The held-out improve_rubric loop stays deferred (calibrate-with-human covers it until labelled sets are large). See the autorubric review note item 6.

title: Penalty criteria: negative weights in checklists (from autorubric review)
description: Adopt autorubric's clamped formula exactly: score = max(0, min(1, sum(v*w) / sum(w>0))); negative weights excluded from the denominator so a perfect response scores 1.0; clamping stops penalties pushing below zero. Pass rule stays strict: every positive criterion holds AND no negative criterion fires. checklistScore changes a few lines; docs must say hard gates remain their own case, not a large negative weight. See the autorubric review note item 5.

title: Design cycle: Wiki effect with grounding-gated writes (from llm-wiki research)
description: Brainstorm-with-user candidate: ReadPage/WritePage/Index/Search/AppendLog effect, scripted + directory interpreters, typed page frontmatter via HasCodec, wiki ops exposed as Tools, and grounding-gated writes reusing Eval.Grounding to verify edits against source traces. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-llm-wiki.md.

title: Design cycle: Spawn effect for orchestrator-worker (from harness research)
description: Brainstorm-with-user candidate: synchronous Spawn with codec-typed handoffs (no surveyed harness has a typed handoff boundary), judge-gated subagent outputs (MAST: 21.3% of failures are missing verification), a small work-ledger effect, spawn-tree budgets. Explicit non-goals: process management, role catalogs, group chat/debate. See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-multi-agent-harnesses.md.

title: Semantic streaming of partial typed values (from BAML review)
description: runPartial interpreter over Emit: close unbalanced JSON in the buffer and decode through a derived all-optional codec, lifting docs/streaming.md's single-object-partial-decode exclusion. See the BAML review note.

title: Design cycle: Memory effect (from agentic-memory research)
description: Brainstorm-with-user candidate: Remember/Recall/Forget effect with scripted/pure/file interpreters, typed memories via HasCodec (free staleness when decode fails), mandatory provenance fields, consolidation as an offline Skill returning keep/merge/supersede/drop, ablation eval hooks (does a memory raise testSkill scores). See docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-agentic-memory.md.

title: Assert/check as codec refinements (from BAML review)
description: refine (hard: fails decode so call's retry loop feeds the violation back, stronger than BAML's raise-to-caller) and checked (soft: Checked wrapper with per-check pass map). See the BAML review note.

title: Abstain verdict for the judge (from autorubric review)
description: VerdictKind = Pass | Fail | CannotAssess with an explicit AbstainPolicy (fail by default, or drop the criterion from the checklist denominator); rendered distinctly from judge errors and counted separately in calibration. See the autorubric review note.

title: Cross-model judge panels (from autorubric review)
description: Pure tally :: [Either JudgeError Verdict] -> VoteOutcome plus votePanel over judge actions run under different interpreters. Fixes documented weaknesses: no per-call temperature means n votes can be n copies of one opinion; evals rule 13 already says judge with another family. Stays open-loop. See the autorubric review note.

title: calibrate docs: human labels are binary task-level only (guardrail)
description: Decision from reasoning-trap research review (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-reasoning-trap.md, point 4). Add a guardrail line to the calibrate documentation when it lands: human calibration labels are binary pass/fail on task-level outcomes; never Likert/numeric ratings of reasoning quality (R6 study: chance-level inter-rater agreement, Fleiss kappa <= 0.018). Critique text accompanies labels as qualitative context and few-shot judge-prompt examples, not as calibration targets. If human signal on reasoning is needed, decompose into observable binary proxies instead.
close_reason: Guardrail added to the calibration workflow in docs/evals.md: labels are binary task-level pass/fail, never reasoning-quality scales; critiques are context/few-shot material, not targets; decompose reasoning concerns into binary proxies. + at-a-glance rule 17.

title: groundingCheck: SFS-style derived claim checklist
description: Decision from reasoning-trap research review (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-reasoning-trap.md, point 3b). Roadmap item, blocked on Checklist landing. Two stages: (1) decompose skill output into atomic factual claims via LLM call; (2) verify each claim against provided evidence with per-claim binary judge calls; Score = supported/total. Complements static checklists: author-written criteria catch missing-expected-content, derived claims catch unexpected/hallucinated content. Design questions: claim granularity; whether passRate's all-criteria-pass default is right for derived claims; new Expectation constructor (Grounded evidence) vs standalone function returning Score. De-risked by paper finding rankings invariant to decomposer choice (Spearman rho = 1.0).
notes: Unblocked (Checklist + judgeN + grounding guidance all shipped). Open design questions per the issue (claim granularity; passRate semantics for derived claims; Grounded Expectation ctor vs standalone groundingCheck) make this a brainstorm-with-user cycle, not autonomous work. Suggest /loop pause here and design it interactively.
close_reason: Shipped: Crucible.Eval.Grounding (decompose + per-claim votes), Grounded expectation + groundingCheck in Eval, 7 hermetic tests, evals.md derived-claims subsection, live-verified demo case (decomposer split the demo sentence into 2 claims, both grounded with quoted spans).

title: Skill prompt upgrades from prompt-performance research
description: Implement the recommendations in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-prompt-performance.md: (1) withReasoning combinator wrapping the output codec as {reasoning, result} with reasoning first, (2) few-shot rendering of attached Exactly cases as example blocks via the codecs, (3) prompt tweaks (machine-parse motivation line, input delimiters, trailing format reminder, field docs in schemaText), (4) retry reprompt restating the schema contract, (5) improveSkill testSkill-driven instruction hill-climb (needs a constant/preamble instruction slot).
notes: Shipped so far: few-shot from cases (withExamples/examplesFromTests), withReasoning, schema-restating retry reprompt, composing-prompts + reasoning docs. Remaining and deliberately deferred: global prompt-text tweaks (machine-parse motivation line, input delimiters, trailing format reminder; they change the core prompt contract broadly) and improveSkill (needs a design cycle: requires a structured/preamble instruction slot since i -> Text is opaque).

title: Eval upgrades from rubric research (judge CoT, Checklist, judgeN, calibrate)
description: Implement the recommendations in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-evaluation-rubrics.md: (1) reorder judge JSON to {why, pass} so the verdict conditions on the reasoning, (2) Checklist [Criterion] expectation with weighted binary criteria, (3) judgeN n-vote self-consistency, (4) calibrate helper comparing judge verdicts to hand labels (kappa, fail-class precision/recall), (5) harden judge prompt against verbosity/lean-to-pass bias.
close_reason: Closed

title: OpenAI: streaming + cassette interpreters, live verification
description: Follow-ups to crucible-luz: (1) OpenAI.stream/streamChat (SSE deltas differ from Anthropic's event shapes), (2) record/replay cassette parity, (3) live smoke verification once OPENAI_API_KEY is available in .env (the module is currently fixture-tested only).
close_reason: Shipped and live-verified: OpenAI.stream/streamChat (SSE event-list parsing, indexed tool-arg reassembly, include_usage), cassette parity via shared Crucible.Chat turn serialization, demo section gated on OPENAI_API_KEY. Live run: typed skill, tool agent, both streams, cassette replay all green.

title: Manual pages for Eval and the text-path Agent loop
description: Crucible.Eval (runEval, scoreM, judge, renderReport, LLM-as-judge) and Crucible.Agent/Crucible.Decision (text-path agent loop) are public surface with no manual page. Write docs/evals.md (and decide whether the text-path agent deserves a page or a section).
notes: docs/evals.md now covers the Eval/judge half (shipped with the rubric upgrades). Remaining scope: a page or section for the text-path Agent loop (Crucible.Agent/Decision).
close_reason: Both halves shipped: docs/evals.md (with the rubric upgrades cycle) and 'The text-path agent' section in tool-calling.md (Decision loop, Tools dispatch, hermetic example, Chat-path comparison).

title: Research: techniques to improve prompt performance
description: Research and write up techniques for improving prompt performance (schema-first prompting, few-shot selection, decomposition, self-consistency, reprompting strategies), with recommendations applicable to Skill/prompt and the retry loop. Outcome: written summary.
close_reason: Written up in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-prompt-performance.md: reasoning-field convention, few-shot from attached Cases, prompt/retry tweaks, testSkill-driven optimization loop; follow-up issue filed.

title: Research: techniques to build evaluation rubrics
description: Research and write up (docs or bd notes) practical techniques for constructing LLM evaluation rubrics: rubric decomposition, pairwise vs absolute scoring, judge calibration, known pitfalls. Outcome: written summary with recommendations for crucible's Eval module.
close_reason: Written up in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-evaluation-rubrics.md: binary weighted checklists over Likert, CoT-before-verdict judge, n-vote juries, calibration vs ~30 hand labels; follow-up issue filed for implementation.

title: Test: autodocodec generic schema drops Maybe fields from required
description: SP3 final-review coverage gap. The migration weakened the schema-shape assertions to type==object/array checks; the old tests verified that optional (Maybe) record fields are dropped from a JSON-Schema's 'required' list. No current domain record uses a Maybe field through genericCodec, so this is latent — but before one is added, add a unit test asserting schemaValue of a record with a Maybe field yields a JSON-Schema whose 'required' omits that field (autodocodec's maybeCodec/optional handling). Surfaced reviewing feat/aeson-autodocodec.
close_reason: Test added and it exposed a real bug: Maybe fields were required on the wire. Fixed with an overlapping optionalFieldOrNullWith' selector instance; 3 tests pin required-list omission, absent-key decode, and explicit-null decode.

title: Streaming: guard against mid-stream idle timeout on long generations
description: The TLS manager's responseTimeout governs time-to-headers, which streaming respects, but a very long streaming generation (e.g. a multi-round tool agent) could in principle stall mid-stream with no per-read/idle timeout. Surfaced in the A#3 SSE-streaming review as an operational follow-up (not a correctness bug). Consider an idle/read timeout on the streamLoop brRead, or document the current behaviour. Low priority until a long-stream stall is actually observed.
close_reason: Shipped: acStreamIdleSecs config (default 60s) + AnthropicStreamTimeout error + timedRead wrapping each brRead in System.Timeout, threaded through streamLoop. Mid-stream stall now aborts (non-retryable) with bracketed responseClose instead of hanging. Merged to master (ae83793).

title: DRY: share request-building + retry between openStream and postMessages
description: openStream (Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.Stream) duplicates ~20 lines from postMessages (Crucible.LLM.Anthropic): the parseRequest+headers+RequestBodyLBS request builder, the recovering retry policy with the AnthropicError handler, and the maxBackoffMicros=30000000 constant. Accepted tradeoff during A#3 (kept the text path untouched + streaming self-contained). Now that openStream/postMessages/anthropicComplete/converseOnce all share the pattern, extract a buildMessagesRequest :: AnthropicConfig -> Value -> IO Request and a shared retry policy/constant into Anthropic.hs and have both call them. Behaviour-preserving refactor.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').

title: Native tool-calling polish: configurable loop cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded
description: Minor follow-ups from the native tool-calling final review. (1) runToolAgent hardcodes defaultMaxIterations (10); make the cap a parameter. (2) ToolLoopExceeded always reports 10 rather than the actual budget used - report the real cap once it is configurable. (3) The loop accumulates full message history with msgs ++ [assistant, results] each round (O(n-squared)); fine at the current bound, revisit only if the cap grows. All non-blocking.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').

title: Chat cassettes: record/replay for the native tool-calling path
description: Deferred non-goal from the native tool-calling spec (2026-06-09). The text path already demos record/replay cassettes; the Chat capability (block-based ChatMsg/Turn, multi-turn tool loops) is richer to serialize and was deferred. Add a recordChatAnthropic + replay interpreter so tool-calling conversations can run hermetically in tests/CI. Pairs with direction C (evals+cassettes in CI).
close_reason: Shipped: turnContentJson (Turn->cassette JSON, round-trips via parseTurn) + recordChatAnthropic/runChatCassette (record/replay for native tool-calling, mirroring the LLM cassettes). Hermetic replay test drives a full runToolAgent tool loop with no network; live demo records+replays a tool-agent run (replay matches). Merged to master (ceb50cf).

title: A#3: streaming (SSE) for the live Anthropic path
description: Direction A, sub-project 3. Consume Anthropic server-sent-events streaming so tokens arrive incrementally instead of one blocking httpLbs call. Touches the shared postMessages HTTP core; likely a new streaming interpreter variant alongside runLLMAnthropic/runChatAnthropic. Larger than A#4. Needs its own brainstorm -> spec -> plan cycle.
close_reason: Shipped: Crucible.Emit (delta effect) + Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.Stream (pure SSE core + runLLMAnthropicStream/runChatAnthropicStream, both returning (a,Usage)). Live-verified: tokens stream live, tool-arg reassembly works, 1271 tokens summed across a streaming tool loop. Merged to master (a51cfd4).

title: Latency assertions in tests (from BAML review)
description: BAML test asserts can reference _.latency_ms. A Case/Expectation wall-clock predicate would only be meaningful under live interpreters and is noise under cassettes/scripted runs; the review ranks it last and recommends low priority. Filed for completeness; consider gating on the CallLog work (timing data source) and an explicit live-only marker in the report. See the BAML review note item 7.

title: Main typed-fn demo prints decode error on live runs
description: In app/Main.hs the typed-fn (llmFn classify) demo prints 'typed fn decode error: expected string, got object' on a successful live API call, because the model replies with surrounding prose/structure rather than a bare word, so the str decoder fails. Pre-existing (predates A#4 usage capture), surfaced during the usage-capture final review. Demo-only cosmetic; fix by tightening the prompt to force a single bare word, or making the demo tolerant. Out of scope for usage capture.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').

value: Prompt-performance research (2026-06-11 note) is now fully implemented in crucible except schema-field-docs in schemaText (genericCodec has no doc source). Shipped across cycles: withExamples/examplesFromTests, withReasoning, schema-restating retry, the three global prompt tweaks (machine line, <input> delimiters, trailing reminder), Instruction{preamble,task,constraints} with skillWith/withPreamble/withConstraints, and Crucible.Skill.Improve.improveSkill (reflector hill-climb, strict meanScore acceptance). Goodhart rails documented in typed-functions.md 'Improving a skill'.

manifest-evals (~/code/garethstokes/manifest-evals) is the bridge project between crucible and manifest: depends on both via pinned git revs in zinc.toml ([dependencies.crucible] rev = ...). Its crucible surface today: Crucible.LLM (Message/Role/complete), Crucible.Usage, Crucible.LLM.Anthropic (config/errors); scoreN was exported from Crucible.Eval at its request. When crucible master moves, bump the pin with 'zinc update crucible' (targeted; full 'zinc update' currently fails on the miso spike's assoc/these registry gap), then 'zinc build .' + 'zinc test' (spins up its own postgres). Crucible breaking changes ripple here first.
